name: Palama Supermarket
tagline: Hawaii's favorite Korean grocery — legendary banchan counter, fresh kimchi, and hot plate lunches that rival any restaurant.
parking: Free lot on N King St
talkStory: Founded in the 1970s, Palama Supermarket became the go-to grocery for Honolulu's Korean community long before H Mart arrived. The banchan counter is the real draw -- a dozen different Korean side dishes ladled into containers for just a few dollars. The in-house kimchi is made fresh from a family recipe, and the hot plate lunches feature Korean classics like kalbi, japchae, and kimchi jjigae that rival dedicated restaurants. Old-timers remember when the Kalihi neighborhood was predominantly Korean.
